akra (Indra) and Jaya, on
the genital organ; Brahman, on the heart; and Pitr, on the feet.
[In verse 53 and would have been better readings
instead of 3^ and In this figure of the House-God there are
nineteen limbs from head to foot, of which chest appears in three
lists viz. Apavatsa as mere chest, and then Indra as the right side
of it, and Bhujaga as the left side of the chest. Only one deity viz.
Pitr, is assigned to the feet. The commentator too has erred while
allotting deities to the various limbs on the left side : The author
has assigned Vivasvat to the right side of the stomach, but Utpala
has forgotten to assign any deity to its left side. Actually Mitra
should be assigned to the left side of stomach. I feel that the origi-
nal reading in the last line must have been, W 5ft instead of...
ypRft. Another doubt arises in mind as I count the limbs taken by
the author i.e. he has not assigned any deity to the neck. So I make
bold to suggest that the reading should be, in
